title: Longevity and Anti-Senescence Therapy Market Projected at $20.7B by 2030
description: North America leads the longevity therapy market growing at 6% CAGR, with increasing clinical investigation of multi-drug combinations like metformin with rapamycin for metabolic and immune function.

title: Yamanaka Factors + AI Fast-Tracking Age-Reversal Research in 2026
description: OSKM reprogramming combined with AI-driven discovery approaches are accelerating cellular clock reset research, with multiple groups advancing toward clinical translation in 2026.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Overview of top longevity biotechs including Altos Labs ($3B funding), Cambrian Bio ($1.79B valuation, $100M Series C), Genflow Biosciences (gene therapy for aging), and Junevity (siRNA cell reset therapeutics).

title: Insilico Medicine and Liquid AI Launch Secure AI for Drug Research
description: Insilico Medicine partners with Liquid AI to launch a secure, lightweight AI platform to streamline drug research and development for longevity therapeutics.

title: Science Corp Aims to Be First BCI Developer With Commercial Vision Restoration Product
description: Neurotech company Science Corp aims to become the first brain-computer interface developer with a commercial product to restore vision in patients with retinal disease.

title: Research Links Circadian Disruption to Inflammatory Microglia and Dementia Risk
description: Animal research connects circadian disruption with inflammatory microglia while new funding explores extracellular vesicle therapy for brain health and aging.

title: Dr. Rhonda Patrick: Ultra-Processed Foods Linked to Worse Behavioral Outcomes in Children
description: Study finds replacing just 10% of ultra-processed foods with minimally processed foods in children's diets was associated with improvements in behavioral, emotional, and mental health scores.

title: Viral Billionaire Deaths Study Highlights Challenges of Communicating Longevity Science
description: Alexey Strygin discusses why his study of billionaire deaths went viral and what it reveals about the gap between public interest and scientific nuance in longevity research.

title: Buck Institute Launches Federated Healthspan Data Initiative
description: Buck Institute launches a federated data initiative linking everyday health signals with aging biology to detect disease earlier and personalize healthspan interventions.

title: Eric Verdin: The Future of Research on Aging is Now
description: Buck Institute CEO discusses CD38 inhibitors that increase lifespan in mice and the disappointing clinical results of NR/NMN supplementation in humans, pointing to more targeted NAD+ restoration strategies.

title: Late-Stage Buntanetap Trial Advances Toward FDA Milestone for Alzheimer's
description: Annovis Bio's late-stage buntanetap clinical trial advances toward FDA milestone in an effort to slow Alzheimer's disease and dementia progression.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytics and NK Cell Research
description: Nature Aging published research showing mitochondrial stress is key to senolytic effectiveness. Aging Cell reports on restoring natural killer cell function in older adults.

title: Next-Gen Senotherapeutics: AI/ML-Driven Strategies for Aging Drug Discovery
description: New review covers AI-driven discovery of novel senotherapeutics using advanced computational techniques to identify and optimize drugs targeting cellular senescence.

title: When Longevity Biotech Becomes Geopolitics: Juvenescence Expands to Abu Dhabi
description: Juvenescence's expansion into Abu Dhabi signals that longevity biotech is maturing from lab science into strategic infrastructure with geopolitical implications.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026: Scientists Shift From Fixing Aging to Preserving Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin will explore aging as a loss of coordination between mitochondria, microbiota, immunity, and metabolism rather than a single defect to fix.

title: Rapamycin Dosing for Longevity: What Emerging Human Research Reveals
description: Review of rapamycin dosing data shows weekly doses of 3-10mg can engage aging pathways without transplant-level immunosuppression. Early trials report improved immune function and cardiometabolic markers.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: The longevity biotech market is valued at $30.7B in 2026, projected to reach $52.8B by 2035 at 6.5% CAGR, driven by maturing epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and organ regeneration technologies.

title: 2026: The Year of Longevity and Owning Your Biology
description: Biological age testing becomes as routine as annual physicals. Advanced MSC therapies are increasingly recognized as the bridge between lifespan and healthspan by combating chronic inflammation.

title: GMP Production Begins for Alpha-Klotho Longevity Protein Therapy
description: Production begins under GMP standards for a scalable anti-aging therapy built around the alpha-Klotho longevity protein, advancing Klothonova's cell-based therapeutic approach.

title: WHOOP Joins $34.5M ARPA-H Initiative to Develop FDA-Grade Healthspan Score
description: WHOOP partners in a $34.5M ARPA-H initiative to develop the first FDA-grade Intrinsic Capacity score using wearable data to predict and measure healthspan.

title: Anti-Aging Therapies Market Targets $420B Milestone
description: Anti-aging drugs segment projected to grow from under $100M in 2025 to nearly $3B by 2040 as senolytics, gene therapies, and regenerative medicine advance. Klothonova begins GMP production of alpha-Klotho longevity protein.

title: ARPA-H Awards $22M to Study Treatment to Slow Human Aging
description: ARPA-H funds seven research teams through its PROSPR program to develop tools and therapies to extend healthspan, including testing whether an HIV drug can quiet chronic immune responses triggered by the body's own DNA.

title: Aging Could Soon Be Reversible, Says Harvard Scientist at World Governments Summit
description: David Sinclair announced at WGS 2026 that researchers are about to test for the first time in history whether aging can be reversed and diseases cured through epigenetic reprogramming.

title: Life Biosciences Secures First FDA Approval for Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: David Sinclair's Life Biosciences received FDA approval for a Phase 1 clinical trial using partial epigenetic reprogramming to restore vision in glaucoma and NAION patients by rejuvenating retinal cells.

title: UW-Madison Launches Human Trial to Test Whether Rapamycin Can Slow Aging
description: University of Wisconsin-Madison researchers are studying whether rapamycin (sirolimus), a drug used for organ transplant patients, can slow aging in humans based on compelling animal evidence.
